content_en:
[10] Bukhaaree [11] Bukhaaree

granted an excellent gift and fulfilled a Wajib (obligation) that was due on him...”

If someone were to ask, “How is Naseehah a right of brotherhood, when one’s faults are mentioned?” The answer is not to feel apprehension when your brother informs you of your faults. He may know something that perhaps you are unaware of, and is compelled to tell you about it out of sheer compassion. It is a way of winning over the hearts of those who are endowed with insight.

When a believer gives Naseehah to his brother in Islaam, he helps him in a matter in which his brother has erred because the believer loves for his brother what he loves for himself. Narrated Mu’adh bin Jabal : ‘When Allaah's Messenger # sent him to the Yemen, he went out with him giving him advice, Mu'adh riding and Allaah's Messenger 32 walking beside his riding beast. The last advice the Messenger of Allaah 38 gave me when | put my foot in the stirrup was that he said:

ry Hae Boh be

‘Make your character good for the people, Muadh bin Jabal!’

Then when he finished he said:

“O Mu’adh! Perhaps, you may not meet me after this year, but perhaps, you may pass this mosque of mine and my grave.”

The Prophet 3 then turned facing Madinah and said:

Do Not Become Angry! Sizer Pines y tie oe 7 fase ay fof € IglS Coe Mpls fe 60 gata) oy WI Jl ol “Those nearest to me are the pious, whoever they are and whenever they are.”""?!

When a believer gives Naseehah to his brother, he is disposing of the right that his brother has upon him. Just as you would not like to see a fault in yourself, and would work to remove it, likewise, you should not like to see that fault in your brother. You must hate to see in him what you hate to see in yourself, hence, you should give him Naseehah to remove that fault as you would have liked to receive Naseehah to have that fault removed from you. Give your brother Naseehah and guide him toward goodness, and take him away from harm.

The Golden Advice Series

In response to the obligation of giving Naseehah and its obvious need in our Ummah at the present time, DARUSSALAM PUBLICATIONS has come up with The Golden Advice Series. which was orginally published by Deen Communications Limited, Nigeria. This series will, Insha Allaah, contain several books; each of which is devoted to an advice given from the texts of Islaam (the Qur’aan and the Sunnah) with its explanation as provided by the esteemed scholars or people of knowledge.

The advice range from the most important issue (tawhid or worshipping Allaah alone), to morals and also issues of business transactions. Each book comes in an easy-to-read size but the contents are highly beneficial, Bi Ithni’Ilah! In the first phase, ten pieces of advice have been treated.

[12] Ahmad (21042) & Muwatta.

Golden Advice Series 03

The book you are holding now belongs to the second print of the first phase. Ten pieces of advice have now been covered and ten books are presented as one handy useful package. It is our hope that, /nsha Allaah, more will be added in the days ahead.

Everything that is correct in the books of The Golden Advice Series is from Allaah — and to Him is all the praise; every mistake or error is from our imperfection and from Shaytaan — and we seek refuge in Allaah from him! We ask Allaah to accept this effort of ours as an act done purely and sincerely for His Face and that He does not allow anyone or anything else to have a share in it. The end of our Du’aa’ is that all praise is for Allaah, Lord of the worlds. And may His peace and blessings be upon the Prophet Muhammad 3%, his family, his Companions and those who follow him till the Day of Reckoning! Aameen.

Ramadhan, 1428 A.H. September 2007

Abdullah bin Amr bin Al-As "*! 4 narrated that he asked the Prophet #: “What might save me from Allaah’s anger?’ He answered:

Cas Y “Do not become angry.’ ""*!

Abu Hurayrah'"*! 2 also narrated:

Aman came to the Prophet 3 and said: “Teach me something that will not be heavy upon me so that | will be able to keep it and

[13] He was from the tribe of Quraysh and was a notable companion just as his father. He embraced Islaam before his father did. He was among the few people who knew how to read and write in the pre-Islamic days. He received permission from the Prophet % to record everything that he heard from the Prophet 48. Infact, Abu Hurayrah 4% once noted that the only one who could have more hadith than him was Abdullah, because Abdullah used to record the hadith while Abu Hurayra did not. He died in the year 65 AH at the age of 72.

[14] sahih At-Targheeb wa At-Tarheeb, no. 2747. Al-Bukhaaree, no. 6116.

[15] Abu Hurayrah % was a famous companion of the Prophet 38 who was born about eighteen years before Hijrah. His real name is Abdul Rahman bin Sakhr. He arrived Madinah in 7 AH, having accepted Islaam much earlier. He constantly kept the company of the Prophet 2%. He lived in the Prophet’s Mosque as one of the people known as Ahi-as-Suffa. He related more hadith than any other companion, having related 5374 Ahadeeth.

Goiden Advice Series 03

memorize it.” The Prophet % told him:

: BEY “Do not become angry and furious.”

The man asked (the same) again and again, and the Prophet said in each case;

“Do not become angry and furious.'16"471

[16] Bukhaaree Volume 8, Book 73, Number 137 [17] Sunan Al-Tirmidhee.

‘Do Not Become Angry’

This is a very short, easy to keep in mind advice and it is of great importance. In fact, in the narration of Al-Bukharee, it comes across that the person himself — just as many of us — did not realize how comprehensive and farreaching this advice is. For that reason, he kept asking the Prophet # for some more advices, as if what he stated was not enough. If he had pondered the implications of the advice that the Prophet #8 gave him, he would have realized that he was given a very comprehensive and important advice.

Golden Advice Series 03

Interpretations of the hadith

Is anger natural and beyond human control? The obvious answer is No! Otherwise, the Prophet # would then be advising the person (and other human beings) to do something that is not within his ability. Clearly, the Prophet 3 and the sharee’ah as a whole would never request something of that nature. Still, there are three possible interpretations for this statement of the Prophet 3¢.'28)

The first interpretation of this hadith is that the Prophet 38 is ordering the person to follow those means that will keep the person from getting angry. That is, a person should learn how to change his character and adopt the characteristics of generosity, kindness, calmness, modesty, patience, forbearing, forgiveness, easygoing nature and so forth. If a person adopts these qualities and he becomes used to such behaviour, then he will be able to control his temper at the time he is about to get angry.

The second view is that what the Prophet meant by that statement is that one should not act based on one’s anger or one should

[18] Note that this advice of the Prophet 3 is only concerning the blameworthy anger. There is such a thing as praiseworthy anger and that shall also be discussed below. There are also cases when anger is justified and one has the right to have his issue resolved. This would be the case, for example, when, according to the Shari’ah, a person’s rights have been violated.

not act while angry. That is if a person becomes angry, he should fight himself and not do the deed that his anger would lead him to do. This is similar to how Allaah describes the Prophet Moosa | who did not act until his anger was over;

a os eS Op

“And when the anger of Moosa_ was appeased...” [Al-Araaf (7): 154]

Thus, it is said that the interpretation of the description of this advice:

“Do not become angry.”

is of a person who gets angry and his anger “tells” him to do something, but then he struggles against himself and repels what his anger is driving him to do, to the point that his anger might even go away. Then it is as if such a person did not get angry in the first place."9!

There is no question that if a person does get angry he must do what he can to restrain himself from acting upon that anger. This type of behaviour is also greatly praised in the Sharee‘ah. The Prophet 3 once said:

[19] Ibn Rajab, Jaami, vol. 1, p. 364.

5 (gd Lah Gy ae Lab 55

“The strong person is not the one who is strong in wrestling. But the strong person is the one who is able to restrain himself when he is angry.”0)

The third view is a more literal understanding of the hadith. It states that when a person is about to get angry, he must remain patient, control himself, calm himself down and not get angry. This is the meaning that comes to mind when one hears this advice of the Prophet #. The person should remain calm and he should handle the matter with wisdom and rationality.)

[20] Al-Bukhaaree (6114) and Muslim (2609). [21] Al-Baitaar, p. 105.

What is Anger? Linguistically, anger is a strong feeling of annoyance, displeasure or hostility.

Technically, anger is an internal change of emotions, which prompts one to attack and seek revenge so as to satisfy one’s spirit. Fury is more intense than anger, so it is defined as wild and violent anger.

Anger is the flaw that has inflicted, and is still inflicting a number of Muslims and has led to most of what we witness today in the Muslim Ummah.

The Features of Anger

Anger has many features and forms some of which include:

1. The swelling of blood vessels and jugular veins in addition to the reddening of face and eyes.

2. Frowning, bringing the eyebrows together, and wrinkling the skin on one’s forehead.

3. Assaulting others verbally or physically.

4. Repaying an act of aggression with a similar or a severer one without considering the consequences.
content_id:
[10] Bukhaaree [11] Bukhaaree

telah diberikan karunia yang luar biasa dan telah menunaikan kewajiban (Wajib) yang menjadi tanggungannya..."

Jika seseorang bertanya, "Bagaimana nasehat merupakan hak persaudaraan, padahal kesalahan seseorang disebutkan?" Jawabannya adalah jangan merasa khawatir ketika saudaramu memberitahukan kesalahanmu. Dia mungkin mengetahui sesuatu yang barangkali kamu tidak menyadarinya, dan dia terdorong untuk memberitahumu karena rasa kasih sayang yang tulus. Ini adalah cara untuk memenangkan hati orang-orang yang memiliki pemahaman mendalam.

Ketika seorang mukmin memberikan nasehat kepada saudaranya dalam Islam, dia membantunya dalam perkara di mana saudaranya telah berbuat keliru, karena seorang mukmin mencintai untuk saudaranya apa yang dia cintai untuk dirinya sendiri. Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adh bin Jabal: 'Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ mengutusnya ke Yaman, beliau keluar bersamanya memberikan nasehat, sementara Mu'adh berkendaraan dan Rasulullah ﷺ berjalan di samping kendaraannya. Nasehat terakhir yang Rasulullah ﷺ berikan kepadaku ketika aku meletakkan kakiku di pelana adalah beliau bersabda:

ry Hae Boh be

'Perbaikilah akhlakmu terhadap manusia, wahai Mu'adh bin Jabal!'

Kemudian setelah beliau selesai, beliau bersabda:

"Wahai Mu'adh! Barangkali engkau tidak akan bertemu denganku setelah tahun ini, tetapi barangkali engkau akan melewati masjidku ini dan kuburanku."

Nabi ﷺ kemudian berbalik menghadap Madinah dan bersabda:

Do Not Become Angry! Sizer Pines y tie oe 7 fase ay fof € IglS Coe Mpls fe 60 gata) oy WI Jl ol "Orang-orang yang paling dekat denganku adalah orang-orang yang bertakwa, siapa pun mereka dan kapan pun mereka berada."[12]

Ketika seorang mukmin memberikan nasehat kepada saudaranya, dia sedang menunaikan hak yang dimiliki saudaranya atas dirinya. Sebagaimana engkau tidak ingin melihat kekurangan pada dirimu sendiri dan berusaha menghilangkannya, demikian pula engkau tidak boleh suka melihat kekurangan itu pada saudaramu. Engkau harus membenci melihat pada dirinya apa yang engkau benci melihat pada dirimu sendiri, oleh karena itu engkau harus memberikannya nasehat untuk menghilangkan kekurangan itu sebagaimana engkau ingin menerima nasehat untuk menghilangkan kekurangan itu dari dirimu. Berilah saudaramu nasehat dan bimbinglah dia menuju kebaikan, serta jauhkanlah dia dari bahaya.

Seri Nasehat Emas

Sebagai respons terhadap kewajiban memberikan nasehat dan kebutuhannya yang jelas dalam ummah kita saat ini, DARUSSALAM PUBLICATIONS telah menghadirkan Seri Nasehat Emas, yang pada awalnya diterbitkan oleh Deen Communications Limited, Nigeria. Seri ini, Insya Allah, akan memuat beberapa buku; yang masing-masing didedikasikan untuk sebuah nasehat yang diambil dari teks-teks Islam (al-Quran dan Sunnah) beserta penjelasannya yang diberikan oleh para ulama terkemuka atau ahli ilmu.

Nasehat-nasehat tersebut mencakup dari persoalan yang paling penting (tawhid atau beribadah kepada Allah semata), hingga akhlak dan juga persoalan muamalah. Setiap buku hadir dalam ukuran yang mudah dibaca namun isinya sangat bermanfaat, Bi Ithni'Ilah! Pada tahap pertama, sepuluh nasehat telah dibahas.

[12] Ahmad (21042) & Muwatta.

Seri Nasehat Emas 03

Buku yang kini ada di tanganmu termasuk cetakan kedua dari tahap pertama. Sepuluh nasehat telah dibahas dan sepuluh buku disajikan sebagai satu paket yang berguna. Harapan kami, Insya Allah, lebih banyak lagi akan ditambahkan di hari-hari mendatang.

Segala yang benar dalam buku-buku Seri Nasehat Emas adalah dari Allah — dan bagi-Nya segala pujian; setiap kesalahan atau kekeliruan adalah dari ketidaksempurnaan kami dan dari Syaitan — dan kami berlindung kepada Allah dari keburukannya! Kami memohon kepada Allah agar menerima usaha kami ini sebagai amal yang dilakukan murni dan ikhlas karena Wajah-Nya dan agar Dia tidak membiarkan siapa pun atau apa pun memiliki bagian di dalamnya. Akhir du'a kami adalah bahwa segala puji bagi Allah, Rabb semesta alam. Semoga shalawat dan salam-Nya tercurahkan kepada Nabi Muhammad ﷺ, keluarganya, para sahabatnya, dan mereka yang mengikutinya hingga Hari Pembalasan! Aamiin.

Ramadhan, 1428 H. September 2007

Abdullah bin Amr bin Al-As radhiyallahu 'anhu meriwayatkan bahwa dia bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ: "Apa yang bisa menyelamatkanku dari murka Allah?" Beliau menjawab:

Cas Y "Jangan marah."[14]

Abu Hurayrah radhiyallahu 'anhu[15] juga meriwayatkan:

Seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata: "Ajarilah aku sesuatu yang tidak berat bagiku sehingga aku mampu menjaganya dan

[13] Beliau berasal dari suku Quraysy dan merupakan sahabat terkemuka sebagaimana ayahnya. Beliau masuk Islam sebelum ayahnya. Beliau termasuk di antara sedikit orang yang mampu membaca dan menulis pada masa jahiliyyah. Beliau mendapat izin dari Nabi ﷺ untuk mencatat segala yang beliau dengar dari Nabi ﷺ. Bahkan, Abu Hurayrah radhiyallahu 'anhu pernah menyebutkan bahwa satu-satunya orang yang mungkin memiliki lebih banyak hadith darinya adalah Abdullah, karena Abdullah biasa mencatat hadith sedangkan Abu Hurayrah tidak. Beliau wafat pada tahun 65 H pada usia 72 tahun.

[14] Sahih At-Targheeb wa At-Tarheeb, no. 2747. Al-Bukhaaree, no. 6116.

[15] Abu Hurayrah radhiyallahu 'anhu adalah sahabat Nabi ﷺ yang masyhur, yang lahir sekitar delapan belas tahun sebelum Hijrah. Nama aslinya adalah Abdul Rahman bin Sakhr. Beliau tiba di Madinah pada tahun 7 H, setelah menerima Islam jauh lebih awal. Beliau senantiasa menemani Nabi ﷺ. Beliau tinggal di Masjid Nabi sebagai salah satu dari orang-orang yang dikenal sebagai Ahl as-Suffah. Beliau meriwayatkan lebih banyak hadith daripada sahabat lainnya, telah meriwayatkan 5374 hadith.

Seri Nasehat Emas 03

menghafalnya." Nabi ﷺ bersabda kepadanya:

: BEY "Jangan marah dan jangan murka."

Laki-laki itu bertanya (hal yang sama) berulang kali, dan Nabi ﷺ menjawab setiap kali:

"Jangan marah dan jangan murka."[16][17]

[16] Bukhaaree Jilid 8, Kitab 73, Nomor 137 [17] Sunan Al-Tirmidhee.

'Jangan Marah'

Ini adalah nasehat yang sangat singkat, mudah diingat, dan sangat penting. Bahkan, dalam riwayat Al-Bukhaaree, terlihat bahwa orang itu sendiri — sebagaimana banyak di antara kita — tidak menyadari betapa menyeluruh dan luas jangkauan nasehat ini. Karena itulah, dia terus meminta nasehat tambahan kepada Nabi ﷺ, seolah-olah apa yang beliau sampaikan belum cukup. Seandainya dia merenungkan implikasi dari nasehat yang Nabi ﷺ berikan kepadanya, dia akan menyadari bahwa dia telah diberikan nasehat yang sangat menyeluruh dan penting.

Seri Nasehat Emas 03

Penafsiran-penafsiran terhadap hadith

Apakah kemarahan itu alami dan di luar kendali manusia? Jawaban yang jelas adalah Tidak! Jika demikian, maka Nabi ﷺ berarti menasehati seseorang (dan umat manusia lainnya) untuk melakukan sesuatu yang tidak dalam kemampuannya. Jelas bahwa Nabi ﷺ dan syariat secara keseluruhan tidak akan pernah meminta sesuatu yang seperti itu. Meskipun demikian, ada tiga kemungkinan penafsiran untuk pernyataan Nabi ﷺ ini.[18]

Penafsiran pertama dari hadith ini adalah bahwa Nabi ﷺ memerintahkan orang tersebut untuk mengikuti cara-cara yang akan menjaganya dari kemarahan. Artinya, seseorang harus belajar bagaimana mengubah karakternya dan mengadopsi sifat-sifat kedermawanan, kebaikan, ketenangan, kerendahan hati, kesabaran, pemaaf, kemudahan bergaul, dan sebagainya. Jika seseorang mengadopsi sifat-sifat ini dan dia terbiasa dengan perilaku seperti itu, maka dia akan mampu mengendalikan emosinya pada saat dia hampir marah.

Pandangan kedua adalah bahwa yang Nabi ﷺ maksudkan dengan pernyataan itu adalah bahwa seseorang tidak boleh bertindak berdasarkan kemarahannya atau tidak boleh

[18] Perhatikan bahwa nasehat Nabi ﷺ ini hanya berkaitan dengan kemarahan yang tercela. Ada yang namanya kemarahan yang terpuji dan hal itu juga akan dibahas di bawah. Ada juga keadaan di mana kemarahan itu dibenarkan dan seseorang berhak agar persoalannya diselesaikan. Ini misalnya ketika, menurut syariat, hak-hak seseorang telah dilanggar.

bertindak dalam keadaan marah. Artinya, jika seseorang menjadi marah, dia harus melawan dirinya sendiri dan tidak melakukan perbuatan yang kemarahannya akan mendorongnya untuk melakukan. Ini serupa dengan bagaimana Allah menggambarkan Nabi Musa 'alaihis salam yang tidak bertindak hingga kemarahannya reda:

a os eS Op

"Dan ketika kemarahan Musa telah reda..." [Al-A'raaf (7): 154]

Dengan demikian, dikatakan bahwa penafsiran dari penggambaran nasehat ini:

"Jangan marah."

adalah tentang seseorang yang marah dan kemarahannya "menyuruhnya" untuk melakukan sesuatu, tetapi kemudian dia berjuang melawan dirinya sendiri dan menolak apa yang kemarahannya mendorongnya untuk lakukan, hingga kemarahannya bahkan mungkin hilang. Maka seolah-olah orang tersebut tidak pernah marah sejak awal.[19]

Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa jika seseorang memang marah, dia harus melakukan apa yang dia bisa untuk menahan dirinya dari bertindak berdasarkan kemarahan itu. Jenis perilaku ini juga sangat dipuji dalam syariat. Nabi ﷺ pernah bersabda:

[19] Ibn Rajab, Jaami, jilid 1, hal. 364.

5 (gd Lah Gy ae Lab 55

"Orang yang kuat bukanlah orang yang kuat dalam bergulat. Tetapi orang yang kuat adalah orang yang mampu mengendalikan dirinya ketika dia marah."[20]

Pandangan ketiga adalah pemahaman yang lebih harfiah terhadap hadith tersebut. Pandangan ini menyatakan bahwa ketika seseorang hampir marah, dia harus tetap bersabar, mengendalikan dirinya, menenangkan dirinya, dan tidak marah. Inilah makna yang terlintas di pikiran ketika seseorang mendengar nasehat Nabi ﷺ ini. Orang tersebut harus tetap tenang dan dia harus menangani masalah itu dengan hikmah dan akal sehat.[21]

[20] Al-Bukhaaree (6114) dan Muslim (2609). [21] Al-Baitaar, hal. 105.

Apa Itu Kemarahan? Secara bahasa, kemarahan adalah perasaan kuat berupa kejengkelan, ketidaksenangan, atau permusuhan.

Secara istilah, kemarahan adalah perubahan emosi internal yang mendorong seseorang untuk menyerang dan mencari pembalasan demi memuaskan jiwanya. Murka lebih dahsyat daripada marah, sehingga didefinisikan sebagai kemarahan yang liar dan keras.

Kemarahan adalah cacat yang telah menimpa dan masih menimpa sejumlah kaum muslimin serta telah menyebabkan sebagian besar dari apa yang kita saksikan hari ini di ummah kaum muslimin.

Ciri-ciri Kemarahan

Kemarahan memiliki banyak ciri dan bentuk, di antaranya:

1. Pembengkakan pembuluh darah dan urat leher di samping memerahnya wajah dan mata.

2. Mengerutkan dahi, menyatukan alis, dan mengerinyitkan kulit di dahi.

3. Menyerang orang lain secara verbal maupun fisik.

4. Membalas tindakan agresi dengan yang serupa atau lebih berat tanpa mempertimbangkan akibatnya.
content_ar: [10] Bukhaaree [11] Bukhaaree granted an excellent الهبة and fulfilled a الواجب (الالتزام) that was due on him...” إذا someone were to ask, “How is Naseehah a الحق of brotherhood, عندما one’s faults are mentioned?” The answer is not to feel apprehension عندما your الأخ informs you of your faults. He may know something that perhaps you are unaware of, and is compelled to tell you about it out of sheer الرحمة. It is a الطريق of winning over the القلوب of those who are endowed with insight. عندما a المؤمن gives Naseehah to his الأخ in Islaam, he helps him in a matter in which his الأخ has erred لأن the المؤمن loves for his الأخ what he loves for himself. The last advice الرسول ﷺ of Allaah 38 gave me عندما | put my foot in the stirrup was that قال: ry Hae Boh be ‘Make your الأخلاق الخير for the people, Muadh bin Jabal!’ Then عندما he finished قال: “O Mu’adh! Perhaps, you may not meet me بعد this year, but perhaps, you may pass this المسجد of mine and my القبر.” النبي ﷺ 3 then turned facing المدينة المنورة and said: Do Not Become Angry! And may His peace and البركات be upon النبي ﷺ محمد ﷺ 3%, his الأسرة, his الصحابة and those who follow him till the Day of Reckoning! Aameen. Ramadhan, 1428 A.H. September 2007 Abdullah bin Amr bin Al-As "*! 4 narrated that he asked النبي ﷺ #: “What might save me from Allaah’s الغضب?’ رد: Cas Y “Do not become angry.’ ""*! أبو هريرة'"*! 2 أيضاً narrated: Aman came to النبي ﷺ 3 and said: “Teach me something that will not be heavy upon me لذا that | will be able to keep it and [13] He was from the tribe of Quraysh and was a notable companion just as his الأب. He embraced Islaam قبل his الأب did. He was among the few people who knew how to read and write in the pre-Islamic days. He received permission from النبي ﷺ % to record everything that he heard from النبي ﷺ 48. Infact, أبو هريرة 4% حين noted that the only one who could have more الحديث than him was Abdullah, لأن Abdullah used to record the الحديث بينما Abu Hurayra did not. He died in the year 65 AH at the age of 72. [14] صحيح At-Targheeb wa At-Tarheeb, no. 2747. البخاري, no. 6116. [15] أبو هريرة % was a famous companion of النبي ﷺ 38 who was born about eighteen years قبل Hijrah. His real name is Abdul Rahman bin Sakhr. He arrived المدينة المنورة in 7 AH, having accepted Islaam much earlier. He constantly kept the company of النبي ﷺ 2%. He lived in النبي ﷺ’s المسجد as one of the people known as Ahi-as-Suffa. He related more الحديث than any other companion, having related 5374 Ahadeeth. Goiden Advice Series 03 memorize it.” النبي ﷺ % told him: : BEY “Do not become angry and furious.” The man asked (the same) again and again, and النبي ﷺ said in each case; “Do not become angry and furious.'16"471 [16] Bukhaaree Volume 8, الكتاب 73, Number 137 [17] Sunan Al-Tirmidhee. ‘Do Not Become Angry’ This is a very short, easy to keep in العقل advice and it is of great importance. In fact, in the narration of Al-Bukharee, it comes across that the person himself — just as many of us — did not realize how comprehensive and farreaching this advice is. For that العقل, he kept asking النبي ﷺ # for some more advices, as إذا what أفاد was not enough. إذا he had pondered the implications of the advice that النبي ﷺ #8 gave him, he would have realized that he was given a very comprehensive and المهم advice. Golden Advice Series 03 Interpretations of the الحديث Is الغضب natural and beyond human control? The obvious answer is No! Otherwise, النبي ﷺ # would then be advising the person (and other human beings) to do something that is not within his ability. Clearly, النبي ﷺ 3 and the sharee’ah as a whole would never request something of that nature. Still, there are three possible interpretations for this statement of النبي ﷺ 3¢.'28) The أولاً interpretation of this الحديث is that النبي ﷺ 38 is ordering the person للاتباع those means that will keep the person from getting angry. That is, a person should learn how to change his الأخلاق and adopt the characteristics of الكرم, اللطف, calmness, الحياء, الصبر, forbearing, المغفرة, easygoing nature and لذا forth. إذا a person adopts these qualities and he becomes used to such السلوك, then he will be able to control his temper at the time he is about to get angry. The ثانياً view is that what النبي ﷺ meant by that statement is that ينبغي للمرء not act based on one’s الغضب or ينبغي للمرء [18] Note that this advice of النبي ﷺ 3 is only فيما يتعلق بـ the blameworthy الغضب. There is such a thing as praiseworthy الغضب and that shall أيضاً be discussed below. There are أيضاً cases عندما الغضب is justified and one has the الحق to have his issue resolved. This would be the case, على سبيل المثال, عندما, وفقاً لـ the Shari’ah, a person’s الحقوق have been violated. not act بينما angry. That is إذا a person becomes angry, he should fight himself and not do the deed that his الغضب would lead him to do. This is similar to how Allaah describes النبي ﷺ Moosa | who did not act حتى his الغضب was over; a os eS Op “And عندما the الغضب of Moosa_ was appeased...” [Al-Araaf (7): 154] وهكذا, قيل that the interpretation of the description of this advice: “Do not become angry.” is of a person who gets angry and his الغضب “tells” him to do something, but then he struggles against himself and repels what his الغضب is driving him to do, to the point that his الغضب might even go away. Then it is as إذا such a person did not get angry in the أولاً place."9! There is no question that إذا a person does get angry he must do what he can to restrain himself from acting upon that الغضب. This type of السلوك is أيضاً greatly praised in the Sharee‘ah. النبي ﷺ 3 حين said: [19] Ibn Rajab, Jaami, vol. 1, p. 364. 5 (gd Lah Gy ae Lab 55 “The strong person is not the one who is strong in wrestling. But the strong person is the one who is able to restrain himself عندما he is angry.”0) The ثالثاً view is a more literal الفهم of the الحديث. It states that عندما a person is about to get angry, he must remain patient, control himself, calm himself down and not get angry. This is the meaning that comes to العقل عندما one hears this advice of النبي ﷺ #. The person should remain calm and he should handle the matter with الحكمة and rationality.) [20] البخاري (6114) and مسلم (2609). [21] Al-Baitaar, p. 105. What is الغضب? Linguistically, الغضب is a strong feeling of annoyance, displeasure or hostility. Technically, الغضب is an internal change of emotions, which prompts one to attack and seek revenge لذا as to satisfy one’s الروح. Fury is more intense than الغضب, لذا it is defined as wild and violent الغضب. الغضب is the flaw that has inflicted, and is still inflicting a number of المسلمون and has led to most of what we witness today in the مسلم الأمة. The Features of الغضب الغضب has many features and forms some of which include: 1. The swelling of blood vessels and jugular veins بالإضافة إلى the reddening of face and eyes. 2. Frowning, bringing the eyebrows together, and wrinkling the skin on one’s forehead. 3. Assaulting others verbally or physically. 4. Repaying an act of aggression with a similar or a severer one without considering the consequences.
